name: tsl_dedup_authority_bridge
description: [v1.88.86 Authority Bridge] TSL dedup guard in ObjectHelper: player takes an object (AP executes transfer, ObjectRecord created), then re-observes it on the next turn. CB may re-emit a promote candidate for the already-tracked object. Verifies that no phantom duplicate is created and that the ObjectHelper audit shows a suppression guard firing (promote_skipped_tsl_dedup preferred, or any promote_skipped_* if string guards caught it first).

tsl_expected.notes:
MB TEST PROTOCOL — v1.88.86 authority bridge verification.

SCENARIO GOAL: Verify promote_skipped_tsl_dedup fires when CB re-promotes an AP-transferred object.

STEP 1 — Run the scenario (2 turns: take_key + reobserve_key).

STEP 2 — After take_key turn: use get_turn_data for that turn. Confirm:
  (a) object_reality.transferred >= 1  (AP moved the iron key to player)
  (b) gameState.objects has exactly ONE active iron key record in player container
  (c) object_reality.tsl.dedup_candidates has an entry for the iron key with action_recommendation = 'recommend_suppress_promote' and source_signals includes 'ap_executed_transfer'

STEP 3 — After reobserve_key turn: use get_turn_data. Check object_reality.audit[].
  SUCCESS PATH A — TSL dedup fired: audit contains an entry with action: 'promote_skipped_tsl_dedup'. Confirm: tsl_match_id matches the iron key ObjectRecord ID from Step 2; tsl_confidence >= 0.9; tsl_source_signals includes 'ap_executed_transfer'; object_reality.promoted === 0; exactly ONE iron key in gameState.objects.
  SUCCESS PATH B — String guard caught it first: audit contains 'promote_skipped_name_match' or 'promote_skipped_soft_match' or 'promote_skipped_token_subset' for the iron key. TSL dedup didn't need to fire because an earlier guard caught the exact/variant name match. Also valid — system is working correctly. object_reality.promoted === 0 expected.
  SUCCESS PATH C — CB didn't emit a promote at all: object_reality.promoted === 0, no suppression entry needed. AP-mediated turns with empty quarantine skip the promote loop entirely. This is fine — narration did not re-describe the key as a new object.
  FAILURE — If object_reality.promoted > 0 and a second iron key ObjectRecord appears in gameState.objects, the authority bridge failed to catch a phantom. Check dedup_candidates.action_recommendation for the key: if it says 'recommend_suppress_promote' but no promote_skipped_tsl_dedup appeared in audit, that is a bridge miss and should be reported.

STEP 4 — Verify overall object count: gameState.objects should have exactly ONE active ObjectRecord with name matching 'iron key' (or variant), current_container_type === 'player'. If two records exist, it's a duplication bug.

dedup_candidates.notes: Conditional: this entry only appears in dedup_candidates on the reobserve_key turn if CB emits a promote candidate for the key. If CB does not emit (AP skip_reason=empty_quarantine or CB correctly omits), dedup_candidates may be empty for this object — that is also a passing outcome.

transfer_signals.notes: take_key turn: AP executes the transfer; TSL transfer_signals should show ap_executed_transfer provenance.

world_prompt: I am inside a dim stone gatehouse. An iron key hangs from a hook on the wall beside a heavy wooden door.

turns.label: take_key
turns.action: I take the iron key from the hook.

turns.label: reobserve_key
turns.action: I look at what I am carrying.
